items.html: However, the descent of Hochma below will not diminish her illumination when the Emanator grows one degree further since the light of the upper Keter fills that place of vacant space. Otherwise, had a vacant space been left there, the light from the Emanator to Hochma would have ceased, and she would even lose the Hochma. It would have been better to leave her in her place. Now, however, when the light of Keter fills the place of that vacant space, there is a passageway for the light of the Emanator to bestow in the root of Hochma, and she does not lose at all.
